description: Fix misleading next-wave prediction card with statistical estimation + confidence indicator, add composite historical prediction curve to chart, add data-update vertical lines behind a settings toggle in the filter toolbar.

userStories.title: Add absoluteToWeek helper and estimateNextWave function
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pure function that estimates when the next wave will start based on historical wave gaps and ARIMA forecast corroboration, returning a confidence level.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `absoluteToWeek(absWeek: number): string` to `src/lib/wave-stats.ts` — inverse of existing `weekToAbsolute`. Use `incrementWeek` from `src/lib/forecast.ts` to step from a reference week to avoid 52/53-week ambiguity

Export new types: `ConfidenceLevel = 'elevee' | 'moyenne' | 'faible'` and `NextWaveEstimate = { estimatedStartWeek: string; confidenceLevel: ConfidenceLevel; confidenceLabel: string; method: string }`
Add `estimateNextWave(waves: Wave[], forecast: ForecastPoint[], series: { week: string; value: number | null }[]): NextWaveEstimate | null`
Algorithm: compute inter-wave gaps from waves array. Estimated next start = last wave end + avgInterWaveGap (in absolute weeks, converted back). Return null if < 2 waves

Confidence scoring — 3 signals, each 0–1, averaged: (1) Gap regularity: `1 - clamp(stdGap / avgGap, 0, 1)`, (2) Forecast corroboration: compute slope of forecast predictedValues, score 1 if rising, 0.3 if flat/declining, (3) CI width: `1 - clamp(avgCIWidth / avgPredictedValue, 0, 1)` where CIWidth = upperBound - lowerBound
Map average score to labels: >= 0.65 'Élevée', >= 0.4 'Moyenne', else 'Faible'
The `method` field should be a French sentence explaining how the estimate was computed, suitable for a tooltip
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The existing `weekToAbsolute` at line 31 uses `year * 52 + w` which is an approximation. For `absoluteToWeek`, a pragmatic approach: `year = floor(abs / 52)`, `week = abs % 52`, adjust if week is 0 or > weeksInYear(year). Import `incrementWeek` from `@/lib/forecast` if needed, or keep it self-contained. The `clamp` helper can be inline: `Math.max(0, Math.min(1, x))`. For the forecast slope, use simple linear regression on the 3 predictedValues or just check if the last value > first value.

userStories.title: Unit tests for estimateNextWave
userStories.description: As a developer, I need comprehensive unit tests for the wave estimation logic so I can trust its behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tests to `src/lib/wave-stats.test.ts` in a new `describe('estimateNextWave')` block
Test: returns null for 0 or 1 wave
Test: returns a valid NextWaveEstimate for 2+ waves with correct estimatedStartWeek based on avgInterWaveGap
Test: confidence is 'elevee' when gaps are regular and forecast is rising
Test: confidence is 'faible' when gaps are irregular (high std) and forecast is flat/declining
Test: handles edge cases (waves spanning year boundaries, forecast with empty array)
Reuse existing `makeWave` helper already in the test file
Create a `makeForecast` helper returning `ForecastPoint[]` with configurable slope and CI width
All tests pass: `npx vitest run src/lib/wave-stats.test.ts`
userStories.notes: The existing test file already has `makeWave` helper and vitest imports. ForecastPoint type is in `src/lib/forecast.ts`: `{ week: string; predictedValue: number; lowerBound: number; upperBound: number }`. For the makeForecast helper, generate 3 forecast points starting from a given week with configurable slope (e.g., values [100, 110, 120] for rising, [100, 100, 100] for flat).

userStories.title: Add getNextWaveEstimate tRPC procedure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a tRPC procedure that returns the next wave estimate with confidence for a given station.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `getNextWaveEstimate` procedure to `src/server/trpc/routers/wave-analysis.ts`
Input: same `stationInput` schema (optional stationId, defaults to NATIONAL_COLUMN)
Implementation: fetch smoothed series, detect waves, generate forecast, call `estimateNextWave(waves, forecast, series)`
Return type matches `NextWaveEstimate | null`
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reuse existing `fetchSmoothedSeries` function already in the file. Import `estimateNextWave` and types from `@/lib/wave-stats`, `forecastWastewater` from `@/lib/forecast`, `detectWaves` from `@/lib/wave-detection`. These are all already imported or partially imported in the file.

userStories.title: Update prediction card to show real estimate with confidence
userStories.description: As a user, I want the 'Prochaine vague estimée' card to show a statistically grounded next-wave date with a confidence indicator.
acceptanceCriteria: In `src/components/wave-stats-panel.tsx`, replace the `forecastData` query and `nextWaveEstimate` useMemo (lines 112-123) with a call to `trpc.waveAnalysis.getNextWaveEstimate.useQuery({ stationId })`
Update the prediction StatCard (lines 177-182) to show: value = `semaine du {formatWeekHuman(estimatedStartWeek)}`, unit = `Confiance : {confidenceLabel}`, info = InfoButton with the `method` text
Card only renders when the query returns non-null data
Remove the now-unused `forecastData` query if it's no longer needed by this component
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The StatCard already supports `unit` and `info` props (see lines 42-63). The InfoButton component is already defined in the same file (line 66). The `formatWeekHuman` function is already in the file (line 28). Simply replace the data source and update the JSX.

userStories.title: Add getCompositeForecast tRPC procedure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a procedure that returns a merged prediction curve from all forecast snapshots, keeping the most recent prediction per target week.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `getCompositeForecast` procedure to `src/server/trpc/routers/wave-analysis.ts`
Input: none (forecasts are national-only currently)
SQL: for each `target_week` in `forecast_snapshots`, select the row with the maximum `snapshot_date`. Use a subquery with GROUP BY + max() joined back to the main table
Return `{ targetWeek: string; predictedValue: number; snapshotDate: string }[]` ordered by targetWeek ascending
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Drizzle ORM doesn't support DISTINCT ON directly. Use a subquery approach: `const latestSnapshot = db.select({ target_week, max_snapshot: sql<string>`max(snapshot_date)`.as('max_snapshot') }).from(forecastSnapshotsTable).groupBy(target_week).as('latest')`. Then inner join the main table on both target_week and snapshot_date = max_snapshot. Import `and`, `eq`, `sql` from `drizzle-orm`. The `forecastSnapshotsTable` is already imported in the file.

userStories.title: Render composite prediction curve on chart
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see historical predictions alongside real data on the chart, so I can assess how accurate past forecasts were.
acceptanceCriteria: In `src/components/chart/wastewater-chart.tsx`, add constants: `PREDICTION_HISTORY_KEY = 'prediction_history'` and `PREDICTION_HISTORY_COLOR = 'hsl(0, 0%, 75%)'`
Query `trpc.waveAnalysis.getCompositeForecast.useQuery()` in the component
Merge composite forecast data into the chartData weekMap: for each entry, set `point[PREDICTION_HISTORY_KEY] = cf.predictedValue`

Add a `<Line>` element for the composite prediction: type monotone, lighter gray, strokeDasharray '3 3', strokeWidth 1.5, no dots, connectNulls, yAxisId 'wastewater'. Place it BEFORE real data lines so it renders behind them
Add chart config entry with label 'Prédictions passées'
Add legend entry in the 'Prévision' group with dashed style
Add tooltip case showing 'Prédiction passée' with formatted value
Confidence bands remain only on the current 3-week forecast portion (do NOT add bands to historical predictions)
The line is always visible (not behind a toggle)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the exact same pattern as the existing FORECAST_KEY line rendering (around lines 559-585 for the Line, lines 209-231 for data merging, lines 417-443 for tooltip). The composite data should be merged in the `chartData` useMemo alongside clinical data. Add `compositeForecast` to the useMemo dependency array. The legend entry follows the pattern of existing entries in the `legendEntries` useMemo. The `hide` prop on Line uses `hiddenKeys.has(PREDICTION_HISTORY_KEY)`. Note from progress.txt: Recharts ReferenceLine/ReferenceArea don't have `hide` prop — use `style={{ display: 'none' }}` if needed, but Line does support `hide`.

userStories.title: Add getDataUpdateDates tRPC procedure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a procedure that returns distinct dates when new wastewater data was ingested.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `getDataUpdateDates` procedure to `src/server/trpc/routers/wastewater.ts`
Query: SELECT DISTINCT DATE(first_seen_at) from wastewater_indicators WHERE first_seen_at IS NOT NULL, ordered ascending
Return `string[]` of ISO date strings (e.g., '2025-01-15')
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use `sql<string>` template for the DATE() extraction: `sql<string>`DATE(${wastewaterIndicatorsTable.first_seen_at})`.as('update_date')`. Use `db.selectDistinct(...)` or raw SQL distinct. The `wastewaterIndicatorsTable` is already imported. Follow existing patterns in the file (publicProcedure, no input needed).

userStories.title: Add chart settings hook and component
userStories.description: As a user, I want a settings button in the filter toolbar to toggle chart display options.
acceptanceCriteria: Install shadcn Switch component: `./node_modules/.bin/shadcn add switch`

Create `src/hooks/use-chart-settings.ts` using nuqs: `useQueryState('updates', parseAsBoolean.withDefault(false).withOptions({ history: 'replace' }))`. Export `useChartSettings()` returning `{ showUpdates, setShowUpdates }`
Create `src/components/filters/chart-settings.tsx` as a 'use client' component
Render a `Button variant='outline' size='icon'` with a Settings icon (from lucide-react) as the trigger for a `Popover`
Popover content: a label 'Afficher les mises à jour' with a `Switch` toggle, bound to `showUpdates`/`setShowUpdates`
Add `<ChartSettings />` at the end of the filter toolbar row in `src/app/page.tsx` (after `<AsOfDatePicker />`)
URL param `?updates=true` appears when toggle is on, disappears when off
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the exact nuqs pattern from `src/hooks/use-as-of-date.ts`. Import `parseAsBoolean` from `nuqs`. For the component, import Popover/PopoverTrigger/PopoverContent from `@/components/ui/popover`, Button from `@/components/ui/button`, Switch from `@/components/ui/switch`, Label from `@/components/ui/label` (may need `./node_modules/.bin/shadcn add label` if not present). The Settings icon is from lucide-react (already used elsewhere in the project). Note from progress.txt: `npx shadcn@latest` doesn't work — use `./node_modules/.bin/shadcn add <component>` directly.

userStories.title: Render data update vertical lines on chart
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see vertical lines on the chart showing when new data was ingested, so I know when the dashboard was refreshed with new measurements.
acceptanceCriteria: In `src/components/chart/wastewater-chart.tsx`, import and use `useChartSettings` hook
Query `trpc.wastewater.getDataUpdateDates.useQuery(undefined, { enabled: showUpdates })` — only fetch when toggle is on
Convert dates to ISO weeks using existing `dateToISOWeek` from `@/lib/chart-utils`
Render as `<ReferenceLine>` components: x = ISO week, yAxisId 'wastewater', stroke 'hsl(200, 60%, 70%)', strokeWidth 1, strokeDasharray '2 4', ifOverflow 'hidden'
Short label at top showing 'MM-DD' (e.g., '03-05'), fontSize 9, fill 'hsl(200, 60%, 60%)'
Lines only render when `showUpdates` is true
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The `dateToISOWeek` function is already imported in the chart component (line 26). `ReferenceLine` is already imported from recharts (line 4). Follow the pattern of existing ReferenceLine/ReferenceArea usage in the chart. Note from progress.txt: ReferenceLine doesn't have `hide` prop — use conditional rendering (`showUpdates && updateDates?.map(...)`) instead. The label `value` should be `dateStr.slice(5)` for 'MM-DD' format. Place the ReferenceLine block after existing event markers in the JSX.

userStories.title: Unit tests for chart settings hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests for the chart settings hook to ensure URL state management works correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/hooks/use-chart-settings.test.ts`
Test: default value of showUpdates is false
Test: setShowUpdates(true) updates state
Tests pass: `npx vitest run src/hooks/use-chart-settings.test.ts`
userStories.notes: Testing nuqs hooks requires mocking or using the nuqs testing adapter. Check how existing hooks are tested — if there are no hook tests, this may need a `NuqsTestingAdapter` wrapper from `nuqs/adapters/testing`. If this proves too complex to set up, write a minimal test that imports the hook and checks it exports the expected shape. The existing test files in `src/lib/` test pure functions, not hooks — this may be a first hook test in the project.

userStories.title: Unit tests for composite forecast merge logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify the composite forecast aggregation works correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Extract the 'latest snapshot per target week' logic into a pure helper function `mergeForecasts(snapshots: { snapshotDate: string; targetWeek: string; predictedValue: number }[]): { targetWeek: string; predictedValue: number; snapshotDate: string }[]` in a new file `src/lib/composite-forecast.ts`
Create `src/lib/composite-forecast.test.ts`
Test: returns latest prediction per target_week when multiple snapshots exist for the same week
Test: returns all weeks when each target_week has only one snapshot
Test: returns empty array for empty input
Test: results are sorted by targetWeek ascending
Tests pass: `npx vitest run src/lib/composite-forecast.test.ts`
userStories.notes: Rather than testing SQL directly (which requires a DB connection), extract the merge/dedup logic into a pure function that operates on an in-memory array. The tRPC procedure in US-084 can optionally use this function on fetched data instead of doing dedup in SQL — either approach is fine. This gives us a testable unit without needing a DB test harness.
